description: Personality adaptation dimensions. Each axis tracks how the user communicates and prefers to be communicated with. Values range from 0.0 to 1.0, where 0.5 is the default-profile baseline.

formality.description: How formal vs casual the communication style should be

humor_density.description: How frequently humor, wit, or playfulness appears in responses
humor_density.low_label: minimal (humor only when it genuinely clarifies)

toward_low: ignores or doesn't engage with humor

detail_level.description: How much explanation and context to provide
detail_level.low_label: terse (just the answer, skip the journey)
detail_level.high_label: thorough (show reasoning, provide context, explain alternatives)

reasoning_transparency.description: How much of the thinking process to expose
reasoning_transparency.low_label: results-only (show the answer, not the work)

technical_depth.description: Assumed technical sophistication of the user

pace.description: Preferred interaction cadence and response length
pace.high_label: considered (longer exchanges, thorough coverage per turn)

autonomy_preference.description: How much the user wants to be consulted vs trusting the agent to decide

adaptation_rules.observation_trigger_description: Record observations at natural breakpoints: before commits, end of work chunks, session wrap-up. Not gated to orchestrated workflows — any Claude Code session counts.
adaptation_rules.calibration_trigger_description: Shift dimension values when min_observations_before_shift new observations have accumulated for a given dimension. Can happen during any session, not just iteration review.
